customers.name: Enterprise Transportation Leader
customers.description: A logistics, supply chain, or transportation executive who needs to move freight reliably across modes while controlling cost, reducing tender failures, and protecting customer service levels.
careAbout: Reliable capacity across contract and spot freight
Predictable landed transportation cost by lane and mode
On-time pickup, on-time delivery, and low facility dwell
Visibility into exceptions before customer commitments are missed
Carrier compliance, insurance, safety, and sustainability reporting
Clean freight audit, allocation, and accrual data
winsThem: A governed TMS and control tower that spans planning, procurement, execution, visibility, and settlement
High tender acceptance with backup capacity before service failures occur
Actionable lane pricing and carrier performance recommendations
Managed transportation workflows that reduce manual status chasing
Accurate financial and service reporting for internal stakeholders
theirFear: Freight disruption that impacts retail shelves, production, or customer delivery promises
Spot-market exposure that makes transportation budgets unpredictable
Losing control because brokers, carriers, warehouses, and internal systems are disconnected

jobsToBeDone.name: Plan and execute a reliable transportation network
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Turn forecasted and live freight demand into carrier awards, tenders, appointments, tracking, exception resolution, and delivery confirmation.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Loads move through the right mode and carrier at an acceptable cost while service commitments are protected.

steps.step: Forecast demand and design lane coverage
steps.description: Translate orders, inventory, production, or replenishment signals into lane, mode, and service requirements.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Network Planning Workspace
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Models demand, lanes, constraints, and service policies before tendering begins.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Contract Procurement and Bid Events
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Captures carrier awards, rates, routing guides, and commitment terms.

media.title: Enterprise Transportation Leader JTBD step 1: Forecast demand and design lane coverage
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Plan and execute a reliable transportation network": Forecast demand and design lane coverage.

steps.description: Offer loads through routing guides, contract carriers, dynamic fallback, brokerage, and spot capacity.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Tendering and Routing Guide
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Automates tender sequencing, acceptance, rejection, and fallback rules.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Spot Quoting and Dynamic Pricing
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Finds market capacity when contract coverage is insufficient.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Carrier Matching Engine
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Selects eligible carriers based on lane fit, service, compliance, and price.

media.title: Enterprise Transportation Leader JTBD step 2: Tender loads and secure capacity
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Plan and execute a reliable transportation network": Tender loads and secure capacity.

steps.description: Track pickup, appointment, in-transit, border, warehouse, and delivery events and intervene before failures cascade.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Real-Time Visibility and ETA
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides live status, ETA prediction, geofence events, and shipment milestones.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Exception Management Workbench
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Prioritizes at-risk freight and guides operations teams through recovery actions.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Appointment and Dock Scheduling
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Aligns facility windows with carrier arrival and warehouse readiness.

media.title: Enterprise Transportation Leader JTBD step 3: Monitor execution and resolve exceptions
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 3 of the job "Plan and execute a reliable transportation network": Monitor execution and resolve exceptions.

media.title: Enterprise Transportation Leader JTBD infographic: Plan and execute a reliable transportation network
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for the job "Plan and execute a reliable transportation network" for customer "Enterprise Transportation Leader".

jobsToBeDone.name: Control freight spend and prove performance
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Audit freight bills, allocate costs, benchmark rates, report carrier performance, and improve network decisions over time.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Transportation spend is explainable, recoverable defects are visible, and better procurement decisions are made in the next cycle.

steps.description: Match invoices, accessorials, fuel, detention, and claims against contracted or quoted terms.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Freight Audit and Accruals
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Validates carrier and broker invoices against rated shipment records.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Settlement, Payments and Claims
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Coordinates shipper billing, carrier payment, claims, and adjustments.

media.title: Enterprise Transportation Leader JTBD step 1: Audit and settle freight charges
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Control freight spend and prove performance": Audit and settle freight charges.

steps.description: Use service, cost, emissions, tender, and carrier data to renegotiate rates or shift modes.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Performance Analytics and Scorecards
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Compares lane, carrier, facility, mode, and cost outcomes.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Rate Intelligence and Benchmarking
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Compares contracted and spot rates to market references and historical outcomes.

media.title: Enterprise Transportation Leader JTBD step 2: Improve the next plan
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Control freight spend and prove performance": Improve the next plan.

media.title: Enterprise Transportation Leader JTBD infographic: Control freight spend and prove performance
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for the job "Control freight spend and prove performance" for customer "Enterprise Transportation Leader".

customerJourneyStories.name: Seasonal replenishment through exception recovery
customerJourneyStories.summary: A shipper launches a peak-season replenishment plan, secures contract and backup capacity, tracks at-risk freight, resolves facility delays, and uses cost and service data to improve the next bid cycle.

stages.narrative: Demand rises and the transportation team needs confidence that key lanes have enough contracted and backup coverage.

media.title: Enterprise Transportation Leader journey stage 1: Trigger
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 1 of "Seasonal replenishment through exception recovery": Trigger.

stages.narrative: The team designs lanes, modes, service policies, and routing guide rules.

media.title: Enterprise Transportation Leader journey stage 2: Planning
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 2 of "Seasonal replenishment through exception recovery": Planning.

stages.narrative: Loads are tendered, tracked, and recovered when carriers reject, facilities delay, or weather disrupts routes.

media.title: Enterprise Transportation Leader journey stage 3: Execution
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 3 of "Seasonal replenishment through exception recovery": Execution.

stages.narrative: Invoices, accessorials, claims, and accruals are validated against operational facts.

media.title: Enterprise Transportation Leader journey stage 4: Settlement
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 4 of "Seasonal replenishment through exception recovery": Settlement.

stages.narrative: Carrier scorecards and lane economics shape the next procurement cycle.

media.title: Enterprise Transportation Leader journey stage 5: Learning
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 5 of "Seasonal replenishment through exception recovery": Learning.

media.title: Enterprise Transportation Leader customer journey: Seasonal replenishment through exception recovery
media.alt: Enterprise journey storyboard for "Seasonal replenishment through exception recovery" for customer "Enterprise Transportation Leader".

top.name: On-time pickup and delivery
top.description: Share of shipments meeting both pickup and delivery commitments.

children.name: Tender acceptance rate
children.description: Share of tenders accepted by awarded or dynamically selected carriers.

children.name: Fallback coverage cycle time
children.description: Median time to secure compliant backup capacity after primary tender rejection.

children.name: Appointment adherence
children.description: Share of pickup and delivery appointments met within facility tolerance.

children.name: Exception recovery before service failure
children.description: Share of high-risk exceptions resolved before customer-facing service failure.

top.name: Managed freight under orchestration
top.description: Annual freight spend or gross freight value actively planned, tendered, tracked, or settled through the platform.

children.name: Net revenue retention
children.description: Revenue retained and expanded from existing shipper accounts.

children.name: Managed transportation attach rate
children.description: Share of strategic shipper accounts using managed service workflows in addition to transactional brokerage.

children.name: Gross profit per load
children.description: Net revenue after purchased transportation per completed load.

children.name: Automation-supported loads
children.description: Share of loads tendered, tracked, or audited with no avoidable manual touch.

productStrategy.vision: Give enterprise shippers a resilient transportation operating system that continuously balances service, cost, capacity, and risk.

1_year.focus: Unify shipper planning, tendering, visibility, exception, and settlement workflows for priority modes.
milestones: Launch unified routing guide and exception workbench for top enterprise accounts
Connect freight audit to live shipment state and documents
Deploy shipper scorecards for lane, carrier, facility, and cost performance

3_year.focus: Move from reactive execution to predictive procurement, automated recovery, and multimodal optimization.

customerKPI.northStar: Planned freight delivered without manual escalation

businessKPI.northStar: Gross profit dollars from contracted and managed freight

milestones: Automate mode and carrier recommendations across truckload, LTL, intermodal, and cross-border
Deploy predictive exception detection and automated recovery playbooks
Build reusable procurement intelligence for annual and mini-bid cycles

5_year.focus: Operate a self-improving logistics network across shippers, carriers, warehouses, customs partners, and financial systems.

businessKPI.northStar: Platform contribution margin from orchestrated freight

milestones: Offer autonomous procurement and dynamic routing guide governance
Integrate warehouse, customs, emissions, and finance loops into shipment decisions
Create network-level performance guarantees for strategic shippers

customers.name: Fleet Owner and Carrier Dispatcher
customers.description: A carrier operator, dispatcher, or owner-operator who wants profitable, reliable freight with low administrative burden, clear facility expectations, fast payment, and fair issue handling.
careAbout: Loads that fit equipment, hours, geography, and profitability
Accurate pickup, delivery, appointment, and facility instructions
Fast payment and clear deductions or adjustments
Low detention, cancellation, and document rework
Compliance steps that are clear and not repetitive
winsThem: Relevant load offers with transparent rate, accessorial, and facility context
A carrier portal or mobile workflow that reduces calls and manual paperwork
Fast document capture, proof-of-delivery validation, and payment status
Fair detention, layover, claim, and cancellation handling
Performance feedback that helps win more freight
theirFear: Accepting a load that becomes unprofitable due to hidden waits, deadhead, or paperwork issues
Payment delays caused by document exceptions or disputed accessorials
Losing access to freight because of opaque compliance or scorecard decisions

jobsToBeDone.name: Find and execute profitable compliant freight
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Choose loads, coordinate drivers, complete pickups and deliveries, provide status, and submit clean documents.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: A load is completed profitably with low operational friction and timely payment.

steps.description: See offers that fit equipment, lane preference, compliance status, and economics.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Carrier Onboarding and Compliance
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Validates authority, insurance, safety, fraud risk, equipment, and eligibility.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Load Offer and Booking
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Shows rate, lane, appointment, commodity, facility, and service requirements.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Carrier Matching Engine
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Prioritizes offers using carrier fit, past performance, location, and preferences.

media.title: Fleet Owner and Carrier Dispatcher JTBD step 1: Qualify and choose freight
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Find and execute profitable compliant freight": Qualify and choose freight.

steps.description: Coordinate dispatch, location updates, facility arrival, detention events, and milestone status.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Carrier Tracking and Check Calls
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Captures mobile, ELD, telematics, and manual status updates.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Appointment and Dock Scheduling
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps appointment windows and facility instructions clear.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Facility Profile and Dwell Intelligence
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides facility rules, risk, dwell patterns, and access details.

media.title: Fleet Owner and Carrier Dispatcher JTBD step 2: Execute pickup, transit, and delivery
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Find and execute profitable compliant freight": Execute pickup, transit, and delivery.

steps.description: Submit documents, resolve accessorials, monitor payment, and use scorecards to win better freight.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Document Capture and Validation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Collects proof of delivery, lumper, scale, customs, and supporting documents.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Settlement, Payments and Claims
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Handles payment, accessorials, disputes, claims, and adjustments.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Carrier Scorecards and Quality
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Explains performance, eligibility, and improvement opportunities.

media.title: Fleet Owner and Carrier Dispatcher JTBD step 3: Get paid and improve access
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 3 of the job "Find and execute profitable compliant freight": Get paid and improve access.

media.title: Fleet Owner and Carrier Dispatcher JTBD infographic: Find and execute profitable compliant freight
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for the job "Find and execute profitable compliant freight" for customer "Fleet Owner and Carrier Dispatcher".

customerJourneyStories.name: Carrier load from offer to payment
customerJourneyStories.summary: A dispatcher receives a relevant refrigerated load, confirms the appointment and facility instructions, tracks the driver, submits proof of delivery, and receives fast payment.

stages.narrative: The dispatcher sees only loads that match authority, equipment, location, and lane preferences.

media.title: Fleet Owner and Carrier Dispatcher journey stage 1: Discovery
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 1 of "Carrier load from offer to payment": Discovery.

stages.narrative: The carrier accepts after reviewing rate, deadhead, appointment, commodity, and facility risk.

media.title: Fleet Owner and Carrier Dispatcher journey stage 2: Commitment
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 2 of "Carrier load from offer to payment": Commitment.

stages.narrative: Tracking and check-call workflows reduce manual calls while exceptions are handled early.

media.title: Fleet Owner and Carrier Dispatcher journey stage 3: Execution
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 3 of "Carrier load from offer to payment": Execution.

stages.narrative: Documents and accessorials are validated quickly so payment status is predictable.

media.title: Fleet Owner and Carrier Dispatcher journey stage 4: Settlement
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 4 of "Carrier load from offer to payment": Settlement.

media.title: Fleet Owner and Carrier Dispatcher customer journey: Carrier load from offer to payment
media.alt: Enterprise journey storyboard for "Carrier load from offer to payment" for customer "Fleet Owner and Carrier Dispatcher".

top.name: Repeat carrier acceptance
top.description: Share of active carriers accepting another load within 30 days after completing a load.

children.name: Relevant offer acceptance rate
children.description: Share of targeted load offers accepted by eligible carriers.

children.name: Facility surprise rate
children.description: Share of loads where carriers report unexpected facility, appointment, or handling conditions.

children.name: Days to carrier payment
children.description: Median days from clean document submission to payment issuance.

children.name: Document first-pass acceptance
children.description: Share of submitted documents accepted without manual rework.

top.name: Digitally covered loads
top.description: Loads covered through eligible carrier network offers without expensive manual fallback.

children.name: Qualified carriers per priority lane
children.description: Median count of compliant carriers with recent activity and lane fit.

children.name: Carrier churn on active lanes
children.description: Share of active carriers not accepting any load in a priority lane for 90 days.

children.name: Buy-rate variance to benchmark
children.description: Average purchased transportation cost variance versus lane benchmark.

children.name: Manual coverage touches per load
children.description: Average human touches required to cover a load.

productStrategy.vision: Make the carrier experience profitable and predictable enough that quality carriers prefer the network.

1_year.focus: Improve load relevance, document quality, payment status, and carrier facility context.
milestones: Launch carrier offer quality scoring
Deploy document first-pass validation
Expose payment and accessorial status in carrier portal

3_year.focus: Use carrier preferences, facility history, and lane economics to build durable supply depth.

customerKPI.northStar: Carrier wallet share on preferred lanes

milestones: Launch preference-aware routing guide and reload matching
Build detention and facility intelligence into offers
Automate carrier compliance refresh and fraud monitoring

5_year.focus: Operate an intelligent carrier marketplace that allocates freight to high-quality capacity before disruptions occur.
milestones: Offer automated reload and backhaul optimization
Use predictive facility and lane risk in offer pricing
Create carrier network quality tiers with transparent benefits

customers.name: Logistics Operations Specialist
customers.description: A broker, managed transportation planner, customer operations specialist, or control-tower analyst who coordinates exceptions, tenders, carrier communication, documents, appointments, and customer updates at scale.
careAbout: Knowing which shipments require attention first
Automating routine tender, status, document, and invoice tasks
Having clean customer, carrier, facility, and shipment context in one workspace
Reducing repetitive calls, emails, and manual rekeying
Reliable operational metrics that show productivity and quality
winsThem: Prioritized exception queues with recommended recovery actions
Unified shipment, carrier, customer, document, and communication history
Automation for tendering, tracking, appointment updates, and invoice validation
Playbooks that reduce variance between experienced and new operators
Productivity metrics tied to service outcomes rather than raw activity
theirFear: Missing the one exception that causes a major service failure
Being buried in manual status requests and low-value administrative work
Losing customer trust because internal systems disagree

jobsToBeDone.name: Run high-volume freight operations without losing control
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Use a control tower to monitor shipments, handle exceptions, coordinate with carriers and shippers, and keep documents and financial records clean.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: More loads are managed per operations FTE while service quality and margin improve.

steps.description: Identify shipments, tenders, documents, invoices, and appointments that need action now.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Operations Control Tower
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Unifies work queues, shipment state, customer commitments, and escalation ownership.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Exception Management Workbench
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Ranks exceptions by service risk, customer priority, margin impact, and time sensitivity.

media.title: Logistics Operations Specialist JTBD step 1: Prioritize the work queue
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Run high-volume freight operations without losing control": Prioritize the work queue.

steps.description: Recover rejected tenders, late pickups, tracking gaps, facility delays, missing documents, and disputed charges.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Workflow Automation and Agents
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Automates repetitive status, tender, document, and customer-update actions.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Customer and Carrier Communications
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Maintains threaded, auditable conversations across email, portal, EDI, API, SMS, and calls.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Document Capture and Validation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Detects missing or inconsistent operational documents before settlement.

media.title: Logistics Operations Specialist JTBD step 2: Resolve exceptions and communicate status
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Run high-volume freight operations without losing control": Resolve exceptions and communicate status.

steps.description: Use analytics, playbooks, and root-cause reports to remove recurring operational defects.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Performance Analytics and Scorecards
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Measures service, cost, productivity, carrier, facility, and customer trends.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Experimentation and Decision Governance
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Tests workflow, pricing, tendering, and automation changes with controlled rollout.

media.title: Logistics Operations Specialist JTBD step 3: Improve operational performance
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 3 of the job "Run high-volume freight operations without losing control": Improve operational performance.

media.title: Logistics Operations Specialist JTBD infographic: Run high-volume freight operations without losing control
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for the job "Run high-volume freight operations without losing control" for customer "Logistics Operations Specialist".

customerJourneyStories.name: Control-tower shift with automated recovery
customerJourneyStories.summary: An operations specialist starts a shift, reviews prioritized shipment risks, lets agents handle routine status work, manually recovers a late refrigerated load, and closes the loop with the shipper and carrier.

stages.narrative: The specialist sees high-risk loads first, not the oldest unread message.

media.title: Logistics Operations Specialist journey stage 1: Triage
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 1 of "Control-tower shift with automated recovery": Triage.

stages.narrative: Automation handles routine updates while the specialist focuses on recovery decisions.

media.title: Logistics Operations Specialist journey stage 2: Action
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 2 of "Control-tower shift with automated recovery": Action.

stages.narrative: Customer, carrier, facility, and document context stays connected in one case.

media.title: Logistics Operations Specialist journey stage 3: Coordination
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 3 of "Control-tower shift with automated recovery": Coordination.

stages.narrative: Recurring root causes are surfaced for procurement, facility, and workflow changes.

media.title: Logistics Operations Specialist journey stage 4: Improvement
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 4 of "Control-tower shift with automated recovery": Improvement.

media.title: Logistics Operations Specialist customer journey: Control-tower shift with automated recovery
media.alt: Enterprise journey storyboard for "Control-tower shift with automated recovery" for customer "Logistics Operations Specialist".

top.name: Exception resolution cycle time
top.description: Median time from exception detection to a confirmed recovery plan or closure.

children.name: Risk-ranked queue precision
children.description: Share of top-priority queue items that truly require action within the next operating window.

children.name: Avoided missed escalation rate
children.description: Share of shipments with detected risk escalated before SLA breach.

children.name: Routine work automated
children.description: Share of routine status, tender, document, and update tasks completed by automation.

children.name: Reopen rate for resolved exceptions
children.description: Share of closed exceptions reopened due to incomplete or incorrect resolution.

top.name: Loads managed per operations FTE
top.description: Completed or active loads managed per operations full-time equivalent while meeting service thresholds.

children.name: Manual touches per managed load
children.description: Average human actions required from tender through settlement.

children.name: Automation containment rate
children.description: Share of routine workflow cases completed without human intervention.

children.name: Service failure cost per load
children.description: Credits, claims, penalties, recovery, and premium coverage cost from preventable service failures.

children.name: Clean invoice rate
children.description: Share of shipments invoiced without manual dispute or correction.

productStrategy.vision: Turn freight operations into a high-leverage control tower where experts focus on decisions that protect service and margin.

1_year.focus: Unify exceptions, communications, documents, and shipment context in one operating workspace.
milestones: Launch control-tower queue across priority accounts
Automate routine status requests and missing document follow-up
Deploy root-cause dashboards for recurring service failures

3_year.focus: Scale AI-assisted planning, exception recovery, audit, and customer communication.
milestones: Deploy workflow agents for tender recovery and invoice validation
Standardize playbooks by shipper vertical and mode
Connect operations quality metrics to commercial renewals

5_year.focus: Move to autonomous operations for routine freight and expert intervention for high-value exceptions.

businessKPI.northStar: Managed freight margin after operations cost

milestones: Autonomously execute low-risk exception playbooks
Use network events to update procurement and planning rules
Offer service-level guarantees backed by predictive operations
